name: npc-sync

metadata.name: npc-sync
metadata.summary: Repackaging of AllenSDK.SyncDataset with extra functionality, compatible with data in the cloud.

documentation:
# npc_sync

Repackaging of `AllenSDK.SyncDataset` with extra functionality, compatible with data in the cloud.

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/npc_sync.svg?label=PyPI&color=blue)](https://pypi.org/project/npc_sync/)
[![Python version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/npc_sync)](https://pypi.org/project/npc_sync/)

[![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/AllenInstitute/npc_sync?logo=codecov)](https://app.codecov.io/github/AllenInstitute/npc_sync)
[![CI/CD](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/AllenInstitute/npc_sync/publish.yml?label=CI/CD&logo=github)](https://github.com/AllenInstitute/npc_sync/actions/workflows/publish.yml)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/AllenInstitute/npc_sync?logo=github)](https://github.com/AllenInstitute/npc_sync/issues)

# Usage
```bash
conda create -n npc_sync python>=3.9
conda activate npc_sync
pip install npc_sync
```
## Python
```python
>>> import npc_sync

>>> d = npc_sync.SyncDataset('s3://aind-ephys-data/ecephys_676909_2023-12-14_12-43-11/behavior_videos/20231214T124311.h5')
>>> d.line_labels[0]
'barcode_ephys'
>>> d.validate(opto=True, audio=True)

# note: some methods from the original `SyncDataset` are now property getters, including `line_labels`
```

# Development
See instructions in https://github.com/AllenInstitute/npc_sync/CONTRIBUTING.md and the original template: https://github.com/AllenInstitute/copier-pdm-npc/blob/main/README.md

modules.name: npc_sync
documentation: Repackaging of AllenSDK.SyncDataset with extra functionality, compatible with data in the cloud.

functions.name: npc_sync.testmod
documentation:
Run doctests for the module, configured to ignore exception details and
normalize whitespace.

Accepts kwargs to pass to doctest.testmod().

Add to modules to run doctests when run as a script:
.. code-block:: text

> if \_\_name\_\_ == "\_\_main\_\_":
>
> : from npc_io import testmod
>   testmod()

modules.name: npc_sync.sync
documentation:
Dataset object for loading and unpacking an HDF5 dataset generated by
    sync.py

@author: derricw

Allen Institute for Brain Science

functions.name: npc_sync.sync.get_sync_data
documentation: Open a path or file-like object and return a SyncDataset object.

functions.name: npc_sync.sync.get_single_sync_path

type.name:
(
        str
        | datetime.date
        | datetime.datetime
        | npc_session.DateRecord
        | npc_session.DatetimeRecord
        | None
    )

documentation:
From an iterable of paths, return the one with the expected date and a
.sync or .h5 extension.

```pycon
>>> get_single_sync_path('s3://aind-ephys-data/ecephys_676909_2023-12-14_12-43-11/behavior')
S3Path('s3://aind-ephys-data/ecephys_676909_2023-12-14_12-43-11/behavior/20231214T124311.h5')
```

functions.name: npc_sync.sync.get_bit
documentation:
Returns a bool array for a specific bit in a uint ndarray.

Parameters
----------
uint_array : (numpy.ndarray)
    The array to extract bits from.
bit : (int)
    The bit to extract.

functions.name: npc_sync.sync.get_sync_line_for_stim_onset
documentation:
Return the sync line index for the given waveform type.

- `opto` currently defaults to laser 488

functions.name: npc_sync.sync.reshape_into_blocks
documentation:
Find the large gaps in indices and split at each gap.

For example, if two blocks of stimuli were recorded in a single sync
file, there will be one larger-than normal gap in frame timestamps.

- default min gap threshold: median + 3 * std (won't work well for short seqs)

```pycon
>>> reshape_into_blocks([0, 1, 2, 103, 104, 105], min_gap=100)
(array([0, 1, 2]), array([103, 104, 105]))
```

```pycon
>>> reshape_into_blocks([0, 1, 2, 3])
(array([0, 1, 2, 3]),)
```

functions.name: npc_sync.sync.adjust_diode_flip_intervals
documentation:
diode flip intervals have a bimodal distribution due to asymmetry of
photodiode thresholding: adjust every other interval to get a closer
estimate of actual transition time for each diode flip

functions.name: npc_sync.sync.add_missing_diode_flip_at_stim_onset
documentation:
Transition from white screen to white sync-square on first frame results
in a missing diode flip (same for black-to-black).
Create a flip after the first vsync with an interval
based on the statistics of other intervals in the
recording.
Shouldn't have any side-effects, and the first frame likely contains nothing important.

functions.name: npc_sync.sync.discard_erroneous_diode_flips_at_stim_offset
documentation:
- after a stimulus the screen usually turns grey, causing
the diode to flip at least one more time after all stim
vsyncs are finished.
- the grey screen itself may be on the threshold of diode activation, causing
multiple additional flips

remove the last flip if doesn't look time-locked with a
vsync (ie. vsync-to-flip interval is an outlier)

variables.name: npc_sync.sync.FIRST_SOUND_ON_SYNC_DATE
documentation:
Prior to this date, there's no sync line with "sound running" signal, on any rig: need to
use NI-DAQ analog recording on OpenEphys PXI to get sound onset times.

variables.name: npc_sync.sync.FIRST_GALVO_ON_SYNC_DATE
documentation:
Prior to this date, opto experiments using the galvo system did not have a "galvo motor
running" signal, on any rig. A copy of the analog signal may have been recorded on the OpenEphys DAQ.

variables.name: npc_sync.sync.MONITOR_CENTER_REFRESH_TIME
documentation:
Best estimate of time from photodiode flip (at top of screen) to updated
content at the center of the screen. Screen refreshes in stages from top to bottom,
in 16 ms: measured by Corbett.

variables.name: npc_sync.sync.CONSTANT_MONITOR_LAG
documentation:
Best estimate of the average time between vsync falling edge and the center of
the screen updating, in seconds. For use when a reliable photodiode signal
is unavailable.

classes.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.validate
documentation:
Check all lines are present and have events.

- if opto or audio are True, work out which line indices correspond and
  check those too

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset._check_line
documentation: Verify line is present and has events, or raise AssertionError.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset._process_times
documentation:
Preprocesses the time array to account for rollovers.
    This is only relevant for event-based sampling.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.load
documentation:
Loads an hdf5 sync dataset.

Parameters
----------
path : str
    Path to hdf5 file.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_bit
documentation:
Returns the values for a specific bit.

Parameters
----------
bit : int
    Bit to return.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_line
documentation:
Returns the values for a specific line.

Parameters
----------
line : str
    Line to return.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_bit_changes
documentation:
Returns the first derivative of a specific bit.
    Data points are 1 on rising edges and 255 on falling edges.

Parameters
----------
bit : int
    Bit for which to return changes.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_line_changes
documentation:
Returns the first derivative of a specific line.
    Data points are 1 on rising edges and 255 on falling edges.

Parameters
----------
line : (str)
    Line name for which to return changes.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_all_bits
documentation: Returns the data for all bits.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_all_times
documentation:
Returns all counter values.

Parameters
----------
units : str
    Return times in 'samples' or 'seconds'

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_all_events
documentation: Returns all counter values and their cooresponding IO state.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_events_by_bit
documentation:
Returns all counter values for transitions (both rising and falling)
    for a specific bit.

Parameters
----------
bit : int
    Bit for which to return events.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_events_by_line
documentation:
Returns all counter values for transitions (both rising and falling)
    for a specific line.

Parameters
----------
line : str
    Line for which to return events.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset._line_to_bit
documentation:
Returns the bit for a specified line.  Either line name and number is
    accepted.

Parameters
----------
line : str
    Line name for which to return corresponding bit.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset._bit_to_line
documentation:
Returns the line name for a specified bit.

Parameters
----------
bit : int
    Bit for which to return the corresponding line name.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_rising_edges
documentation:
Returns the counter values for the rizing edges for a specific bit or
    line.

Parameters
----------
line : str
    Line for which to return edges.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_edges
documentation:
Utility function for extracting edge times from a line

Parameters
----------
kind : One of "rising", "falling", or "all". Should this method return
    timestamps for rising, falling or both edges on the appropriate
    line
keys : These will be checked in sequence. Timestamps will be returned
    for the first which is present in the line labels
units : one of "seconds", "samples", or "indices". The returned
    "time"stamps will be given in these units.
raise_missing : If True and no matching line is found, a KeyError will
    be raised

Returns
-------
An array of edge times. If raise_missing is False and none of the keys
    were found, returns None.

Raises
------
KeyError : none of the provided keys were found among this dataset's
    line labels

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_falling_edges
documentation:
Returns the counter values for the falling edges for a specific bit
    or line.

Parameters
----------
line : str
    Line for which to return edges.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_nearest
documentation:
For all values of the source line, finds the nearest edge from the
    target line.

By default, returns the indices of the target edges.

Args:
    source (str, int): desired source line
    target (str, int): desired target line
    source_edge [Optional(str)]: "rising" or "falling" source edges
    target_edge [Optional(str): "rising" or "falling" target edges
    direction (str): "previous" or "next". Whether to prefer the
        previous edge or the following edge.
    units (str): "indices"

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_analog_channel
documentation:
Returns the data from the specified analog channel between the
    timepoints.

Args:
    channel (int, str): desired channel index or label
    start_time (Optional[float]): start time in seconds
    stop_time (Optional[float]): stop time in seconds
    downsample (Optional[int]): downsample factor

Returns:
    ndarray: slice of data for specified channel

Raises:
    KeyError: no analog data present

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.get_analog_meta
documentation: Returns the metadata for the analog data.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.line_stats
documentation:
Quick-and-dirty analysis of a bit.

##TODO: Split this up into smaller functions.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.period
documentation: Returns a dictionary with avg, min, max, and st of period for a line.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.frequency
documentation: Returns the average frequency of a line.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.duty_cycle
documentation:
Doesn't work right now.  Freezes python for some reason.

Returns the duty cycle of a line.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.stats
documentation:
Quick-and-dirty analysis of all bits.  Prints a few things about each
    bit where events are found.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.stim_running_edges
documentation:
Rising edges and falling edges on stim_running line, filtered for
erroneous events and guaranteed to be of equal length.

- excludes falling edges alone at start, and rising edges alone at end
- excludes rising+falling pairs that contain exactly one vsync events:
    we have one session (DRpilot_726088_20240618) where a stim was cancelled
    immediately after starting and has no data, but it has a short stim
    running block which causes problems

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.filter_on_stim_running
documentation:
Filter data to only include times when stim_running is high.

Data must be in seconds relative to first sample.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.divide_into_stim_running_blocks
documentation:
Divide data into blocks corresponding to stim_running being high.

Data must be in seconds relative to first sample.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.vsync_times_in_blocks
documentation:
Blocks of vsync falling edge times, in seconds relative to first
sample: one block per stimulus.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.expected_diode_flip_rate
documentation:
Best-guess at what the diode flip period should be, e.g. 1 s for
MPE/pipeline recordings, 1/60 s for Sam's TaskControl scripts.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.expected_frame_display_rate
documentation:
Best-guess at what the screen display rate should be. Currently
[2023] should only be 60 fps.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.frame_display_time_blocks
documentation: Blocks of adjusted diode times: one block per stimulus.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.constant_lag_frame_display_time_blocks
documentation:
Blocks of vsync times + a constant: one block per stimulus. For use
when a reliable photodiode signal is unavailable.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.plot_all
documentation:
Plot all active bits.

Yikes.  Come up with a better way to show this.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.plot_bits
documentation: Plots a list of bits.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.plot_bit
documentation: Plots a specific bit at a specific time period.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.plot_line
documentation: Plots a specific line at a specific time period.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.plot_lines
documentation: Plots specific lines at a specific time period.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.plot_diode_measured_sync_square_flips
documentation: Plot the diode-measured sync-square changes that should occur every 1 s while stim is running.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.close
documentation: Closes the dataset.

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.__enter__
documentation:
So we can use context manager (with...as) like any other open file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> with Dataset('my_data.h5') as d: # doctest: +SKIP
...     d.stats()
```

methods.name: npc_sync.sync.SyncDataset.__exit__
documentation: Exit statement for context manager.

A sync dataset. Contains methods for loading

: and parsing the binary data.

# Parameters

path

: Path to HDF5 file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> dset = SyncDataset('s3://aind-ephys-data/ecephys_676909_2023-12-14_12-43-11/behavior/20231214T124311.h5')
>>> dset.validate(opto=True, audio=True)
```

```pycon
>>> with SyncDataset('my_h5_file.h5') as d: # doctest: +SKIP
...     dset.validate()
```

The sync file documentation from MPE can be found at
sharepoint > Instrumentation > Shared Documents > Sync_line_labels_discussion_2020-01-27-.xlsx # NOQA E501
